subject: Public Health
title: COVAX and Vaccine Equity
description: A tiny lesson with a big payoff: COVAX and Vaccine Equity.

hook.text: During COVID-19, rich countries hoarded vaccines while poor countries waited—exposing global inequality.

buildup.text: COVAX was created to distribute COVID vaccines fairly—aiming to cover 20% of each country's population.

discovery.text: Wealthy nations secured enough doses to vaccinate their populations multiple times over—before COVAX could deliver.

twist.text: By the time vaccines reached low-income countries, many doses were near expiration and demand had shifted.

climax.text: The pandemic proved that vaccine nationalism costs everyone—variants emerged in under-vaccinated populations.

punchline.text: No one is safe until everyone is safe. Hoarding helps no one.

quiz.question: What was COVAX designed to do?
options: Distribute COVID vaccines fairly to all countries
